issues.description: The new code reads `msg.metadata`, but the `ToolCall` interface in `gui/sidecar/src/types.ts` only declares `type`, `id`, `tool_name`, `tool_input`. There is no `metadata` field on the wire protocol, so this is always `undefined` at runtime and a TypeScript compile error under strict checking. The fallback `msg.metadata || {}` silently swallows the bug and stores `{}` for every tool call.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) drop the `metadata` field entirely from this change, or (b) first add `metadata?: Record<string, unknown>` to the `ToolCall` interface in `sidecar/src/types.ts` AND have the sidecar emit it before consuming it on the GUI side.

issues.description: The new `toolCall` object literal includes a `metadata` field, but `ToolCallInfo` (lines 10-16 of the same file) declares only `id`, `toolName`, `toolInput`, `status`, `result`. Pushing this object into `m.toolCalls` is a TypeScript error and the field will never be accessible to consumers.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `metadata?: Record<string, unknown>` to `ToolCallInfo` if metadata is genuinely needed by the UI, or remove the field. Do not add data members that no consumer reads.

issues.description: The new code references `msg.tool_call_id`, `msg.output`, and `msg.is_error`. The `ToolResult` interface in `sidecar/src/types.ts` (lines 47-52) declares only `type`, `id`, `tool_name`, `result`. None of those three field names exist on the wire format, so at runtime: `resultCache[undefined] = { output: undefined, is_error: undefined }` — every tool result overwrites the same `"undefined"` key with an empty payload.
issues.suggested_fix: Use the correct existing fields (`msg.tool_name` and `msg.result`) and key the cache by something locally meaningful (e.g. the synthesized tool-call id), or extend the wire protocol in `sidecar/src/types.ts` to actually carry `tool_call_id`, `output`, and `is_error` and update the sidecar emitters to populate them.

issues.description: `m.resultCache = resultCache` writes to a property that does not exist on the `AssistantMessage` interface (lines 18-27 of this file). This is a TypeScript compile error and creates an untyped, untracked field on the store that no consumer can safely read.
issues.suggested_fix: Either add `resultCache?: Record<string, { output: unknown; is_error: boolean }>` to `AssistantMessage` (and to a corresponding renderer that actually uses it), or — preferably — remove `resultCache` entirely (see duplication finding below).

issues.description: `ToolCallInfo.result` already stores the tool result on the matching tool call (the unchanged code below the diff still does `tc.result = msg.result`). Introducing a second `resultCache` map duplicates this information, creates two sources of truth that can drift, and bloats every assistant message with redundant payloads. There is no consumer of `resultCache` anywhere in the GUI tree (verified by search) — this is dead state from the moment it lands.
issues.suggested_fix: Drop the `resultCache` addition. If a quick lookup by id is needed, expose a derived selector that builds it lazily from `toolCalls`, or memoize one if profiling shows the linear scan matters.

issues.description: The original code computed `${msg.id}-tc-${m.toolCalls.length}` inside `produce`, where `m.toolCalls` is the up-to-date draft. The new code reads the length from `state.messages[idx]` BEFORE any setState in this call, then assigns it to a const captured in the closure. For a single message this is equivalent, but the change loses the invariant that the index is computed against the actual array being mutated. Combined with the `(state.messages[idx] as any).toolCalls.length` cast, any future refactor that batches multiple tool_call messages together will produce duplicate ids — and `tool_result FIFO matching` test in `__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts:278-298` asserts ids are unique. Reverting to the in-`produce` computation is both shorter and safer.
issues.suggested_fix: Compute the id inside the `produce` callback: `m.toolCalls.push({ id: `${msg.id}-tc-${m.toolCalls.length}`, ... })` — exactly as the original did. This eliminates the cast and the redundant role re-check.

issues.description: No tests cover the new `metadata` propagation or `resultCache` population. The existing `tool_result FIFO matching` suite (lines 205-355) would not catch any of the type errors above because it uses `as OutboundMessage` casts that bypass type checking. If the new behavior were genuinely intended, it should be exercised by tests; the absence of tests is consistent with this being incomplete or accidental code.
issues.suggested_fix: If `metadata`/`resultCache` are dropped (recommended), no new tests are needed. If they are kept, add tests asserting `metadata` survives a roundtrip and that `resultCache` is keyed/populated correctly — and remove the `as OutboundMessage` casts from those tests so type errors actually surface.

issues.description: Reading `state.messages[idx]` outside `produce()` and then writing the result inside is an anti-pattern with Solid's mutable-draft store. The whole point of `produce` is that you can mutate the draft directly; pre-computing a fresh object via spread allocates redundantly and makes future readers wonder whether the precomputed value can drift from the draft. The surrounding handlers (`text_delta`, `done`, `error`) all mutate inside `produce`.
issues.suggested_fix: Move the cache build inside the `produce` callback and mutate `m.resultCache` (if kept) directly, e.g. `m.resultCache ??= {}; m.resultCache[key] = value;`.

issues.description: Both new blocks use `(state.messages[idx] as any).toolCalls` / `.resultCache` even though `findAssistantIdx` already guarantees the message is an assistant. The `as any` casts are how the type errors above slip past tsc — they hide the very mismatch a reviewer or compiler would otherwise catch.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a typed narrowing (`const m = state.messages[idx]; if (m?.role !== 'assistant') break;`) or do all access inside `produce`, where `m` is already `ChatMessage` and the existing `if (m.role !== 'assistant') return;` guard narrows it correctly.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff introduces a `metadata` field on tool calls and a `resultCache` map on assistant messages, but every new field references wire-protocol or domain-type members that do not exist (`msg.metadata`, `msg.tool_call_id`, `msg.output`, `msg.is_error`, `AssistantMessage.resultCache`, `ToolCallInfo.metadata`). The `as any` casts mask the type errors at compile time but at runtime the new code would silently store `undefined`-keyed empty payloads in `resultCache` and unreachable `metadata: {}` on every tool call. `resultCache` also duplicates information already on `ToolCallInfo.result`, has no GUI consumer (verified by repo-wide search), and is never tested. The pre-`produce` state reads are an anti-pattern relative to the surrounding handlers. Recommended fix: revert both blocks to the original in-`produce` mutation pattern and only reintroduce `metadata`/`resultCache` after extending the sidecar wire types and the `ChatMessage`/`ToolCallInfo` interfaces, with consumers and tests landing in the same change.
